full_response:
We are given  

Ricci tensor  R_{ab}= -\dfrac{3}{l^{2}}\,g_{ab},  
Ricci scalar  R = -\dfrac{12}{l^{2}}.

The Einstein tensor is defined by  

G_{ab}= R_{ab}-\dfrac12\,R\,g_{ab}.  (1)

Insert the given curvature data into (1):

G_{ab}= \Bigl(-\dfrac{3}{l^{2}}\Bigr) g_{ab}-\dfrac12\Bigl(-\dfrac{12}{l^{2}}\Bigr) g_{ab}
      = -\dfrac{3}{l^{2}}\,g_{ab}+ \dfrac{6}{l^{2}}\,g_{ab}
      = \dfrac{3}{l^{2}}\,g_{ab}.  (2)

Hence the Einstein tensor is simply a positive constant times the metric.

\boxed{\,G_{ab}= \dfrac{3}{l^{2}}\; g_{ab}\,}

Confidence: 93%
